commit.message:
feat(prometheus): split parser confidence into core and context-penalty channels (#136)

* feat(prometheus): split parser confidence into core and context-penalty channels

- Add parserCoreConfidence: structural signals only (plansShape base, healthField, requestBudget)
- Add parserContextPenalty: context/external signals (bottleneckCoverage, architectureDrift)
- parserConfidence remains aggregate = max(0.1, parserCoreConfidence - parserContextPenalty) — backward compat
- Drift penalty (architectureDrift) now also increments parserContextPenalty when applied post-parse
- Add 4 new tests covering channel isolation, invariant, and negative paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(prometheus): use resolved canonical health in parser confidence scoring

Replace raw field-only presence check with 3-tier resolved canonical health
scoring in normalizePrometheusParsedOutput:

- healthField = 1.0: model provided an explicit canonical or alias value
  (no penalty, unchanged behaviour for valid inputs)
- healthField = 0.9: field was absent/unrecognized but projectHealth was
  successfully resolved via inferProjectHealth (lighter penalty
  health_field_inferred_from_text, delta -0.1 instead of -0.2)
- healthField = 0.8: unresolvable (defensive; currently unreachable because
  inferProjectHealth always returns a canonical default)

This ensures cycles where the LLM omits the projectHealth field but the
analysis text carries a recognisable health signal are not over-penalised.
All 3549 tests pass; zero new lint errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement A2UI v0.8 spec-compliant improvements

- Path-based DataModel bindings: server transforms literalString → path
  references, data separated into dataModelUpdate operations
- Chunked surfaceUpdate: components split into batches of 15 for
  progressive streaming
- Progressive rendering: client updates UI incrementally as each
  A2UI operation arrives instead of waiting for Done
- Event round-trip: /event endpoint processes userAction events,
  sends to LLM, streams back new A2UI operations via SSE
- Operation reordering: text → begin → data → surface chunks → done
  ensures paths resolve immediately when components render
- Interactive widgets: added TextField, CheckBox, List to system prompt
- Loading indicator: LinearProgressIndicator on A2UI surface during stream

PLASMA: fix PMI crash - re-expand CS after DPMI init calls

Root cause: PMODETSR reverts CS.limit back to the original program size
(0x17ffff) on every DPMI real-mode simulation (INT 31h AX=0300h). Each
VBE INT 10h call during setup (mode set, palette, font query etc.) causes
PMODETSR to save/restore the PM descriptor state, resetting CS.limit.

expand_cs_to_4gb() succeeded at startup, but the last VBE INT 10h init
call (get_bios_font_8x8 / vbe_set_palette) reset CS.limit before the
render loop. Subsequent pmi_set_display_start() called _pmi_call4() with
ESI=0xffcca42c (PMI BIOS entry), which exceeded CS.limit → #GP.

Fix: re-call expand_cs_to_4gb() right before the render loop starts,
after all DPMI real-mode init calls are complete. No DPMI real-mode calls
are made inside the render loop (only direct PMI near-calls and port I/O),
so CS.limit stays at 4 GB for the entire session. If re-expansion fails,
g_pmi_ok is cleared and the demo falls back to VBE INT 10h page flipping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
